steps.title: Locate the singular subject
steps.content:
The subject is text, a singular noun. The prepositional phrase In the installation introduces the sentence but is not the subject.

The participial phrase culled from a series of in-depth interviews with employees and their families modifies text but doesn't change its number — text remains singular.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
The verb must be singular to match the subject text.

Only punctuates (the remaining alternative) is singular; the other choices are plural, passive, or a participle form.

The verb punctuates correctly shows the text interrupting the visual display of the prints.

That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
